title: marketing — published events
description: Every event implements `ShouldDispatchAfterCommit` so listeners never fire against a rolled-back write. MarketingDeadLetterCreated + MarketingProviderCircuitBreakerOpened are the CRITICAL events of the module — the former signals a permanent conversion loss (financial impact); the latter signals a provider outage that will accumulate loss until reset. Both page the on-call. MarketingEventSuppressedByConsent is a compliance signal (spike detection); MarketingDeliveryRateLimited is an ops signal (provider-side throttling).

events.name: MarketingEventCaptured
events.trigger: MarketingEventObserver.creating committed a new marketing_events row. Fires exactly once per (tenant_id, deduplication_key) tuple. Precedes MarketingEventDispatched by the DispatchMarketingEventJob run.

events.name: MarketingEventDispatched
events.trigger: MarketingEventDispatcher enqueued one DispatchToProviderJob per eligible provider. Fires ONCE per event. Distinct from MarketingDeliveryAttempted (per-provider-per-attempt).

events.name: MarketingEventDelivered
events.trigger: Every eligible provider delivery succeeded (delivered_provider_count == eligible_provider_count). Terminal state — event.status transitions to 'delivered'.

events.name: MarketingEventPartiallyDelivered
events.trigger: At least one provider succeeded AND at least one failed permanently (dead-lettered). Event.status = 'partially_delivered'. Distinct from MarketingEventFailed (all providers failed).

events.name: MarketingEventFailed
events.trigger: Every eligible provider dead-lettered. Event.status = 'failed'. Terminal.
consumers: audit::WriteToAuditLog (retention: 7 years — every failed conversion is a compliance-relevant financial signal)

events.name: MarketingEventSuppressedByConsent
events.trigger: MarketingEventObserver.creating detected that the subject lacks `marketing` or `advertising` consent. Refuses capture. Event.status = 'suppressed_by_consent'. This is the CAPTURE-time gate; distinct from MarketingDeliverySuppressedByConsent (dispatch-time re-check on a captured event).
consumers: audit::WriteToAuditLog (compliance record — every suppressed capture is a consent-honored event)

events.name: MarketingEventSuperseded
events.trigger: A newer event with the same (tenant_id, deduplication_key) arrived while the earlier event was still in-flight. The observer marks the earlier event.status = 'superseded' + cancels its pending DispatchToProviderJob. Only fires when the old event has NOT yet delivered — delivered events are immutable.

events.name: MarketingDeliveryAttempted
events.trigger: DispatchToProviderJob started an attempt against a provider. Fires ONCE per (marketing_event, provider, attempt_number). Precedes MarketingDeliverySucceeded or MarketingDeliveryFailed.

events.name: MarketingDeliverySucceeded
events.trigger: Provider returned 2xx AND the response passed provider-specific validation (e.g. Meta's `events_received == 1`). Delivery.status = 'succeeded'. Resets provider's consecutive_failure_count to 0.
consumers: marketing::MaybeCloseCircuitBreaker (checks if in half-open state; closes on success)

events.name: MarketingDeliveryFailed
events.trigger: Provider returned 4xx/5xx OR request timed out OR response validation failed. Delivery.status = 'failed'. Increments provider's consecutive_failure_count. Schedules next retry when attempt_number < max_attempts + is_transient.
consumers: marketing::MaybeOpenCircuitBreaker (checks if consecutive_failure_count >= threshold; opens the breaker)
marketing::MaybeCreateDeadLetter (when attempt_number == max_attempts OR is_transient=false — creates the MarketingDeadLetter row)

payload.next_retry_at: ISO 8601 | null (null = terminal failure, no retry)

events.name: MarketingDeliverySuppressedByConsent
events.trigger: DispatchToProviderJob started + the pre-dispatch ConsentGate::allows check returned false. Delivery.status = 'suppressed_by_consent'. Never touches the wire. Fires when the user revoked consent between capture and dispatch.

payload.revoked_between_capture_and_dispatch: boolean (true when consent was valid at capture but revoked by dispatch)

events.name: MarketingDeliverySuppressedByCircuitBreaker
events.trigger: DispatchToProviderJob started + the pre-dispatch CircuitBreakerGate check found the (tenant, provider) circuit is OPEN. Delivery.status = 'suppressed_by_circuit_breaker'. Never touches the wire. Retries scheduled when the circuit half-opens.

events.name: MarketingDeliveryRateLimited
events.trigger: Provider returned a 429 or the pre-dispatch RateLimitGate refused the send. Delivery.status = 'rate_limited'. Schedules a retry with the provider's `Retry-After` header (or the configured rate_limit backoff).
consumers: monitoring::MetricsCollector (increments academorix.marketing.deliveries.rate_limited_total; spike indicates tenant ceiling breached)

events.name: MarketingProviderCircuitBreakerOpened
events.trigger: CRITICAL. consecutive_failure_count reached failure_threshold. Sets circuit_breaker_open_until = now() + open_duration_seconds. Blocks all subsequent dispatches until the timer elapses. Every fire is a P1 signal — the provider is dead + attribution reporting will degrade until reset.

events.name: MarketingProviderCircuitBreakerClosed
events.trigger: Provider recovered — half-open probe succeeded OR ops manually reset via marketing:reset-circuit-breaker command / POST /providers/{provider}/reset-circuit-breaker. Resets consecutive_failure_count to 0 + increments circuit_breaker_reset_count when the close was manual.

payload.was_auto_close: boolean (true = half-open probe succeeded; false = manual reset)
payload.closed_by_user_id: ULID | null (null = auto-close; set = manual reset)

events.name: MarketingProviderCircuitBreakerHalfOpen
events.trigger: The open_until timer elapsed. HalfOpenCircuitBreakerJob transitions the breaker to HALF_OPEN + dispatches 1 test event (half_open_test_events). Success closes the breaker; failure re-opens it.

payload.test_event_id: ULID (mev_*) — the event used for the probe

events.name: MarketingProviderConfigured
events.trigger: MarketingProviderConfigObserver committed a new provider config row.
consumers: audit::WriteToAuditLog (retention: 7 years — provider connection is a compliance-relevant integration)

events.name: MarketingProviderDeactivated
events.trigger: Provider config is_active flipped to false OR the row is soft-deleted. Stops all future dispatches; in-flight retries drain but no new attempts.

events.name: MarketingDeadLetterCreated
events.trigger: CRITICAL. A delivery exhausted its retry budget OR failed permanently (auth_failure, invalid_payload). DeadLetterHandler created the MarketingDeadLetter row. Every fire is a P1 — the event is a paid conversion signal that never reached the ad network.

events.name: MarketingDeadLetterReplayed
events.trigger: Ops or tenant admin invoked POST /dead-letters/{id}/replay OR the marketing:replay-dead-letter command. Enqueues a fresh DispatchToProviderJob with attempt_number=1. Increments dead_letter.replay_attempts + sets last_replay_at.

events.name: MarketingDeadLetterResolved
events.trigger: Either (a) a replay attempt succeeded (auto-resolved) OR (b) ops manually marked the dead letter resolved via POST /dead-letters/{id}/resolve. Sets resolved_at + resolved_by_user_id + resolution_note.

payload.resolved_by_user_id: ULID | null (null = auto-resolved on replay success)

events.name: MarketingProviderTestSent
events.trigger: Tenant onboarding flow. POST /providers/{provider}/test invoked OR test_mode=true on a new config. Dispatches a synthetic test event via the provider's sandbox endpoint.

events.name: MarketingProviderTestSucceeded
events.trigger: Test-mode dispatch returned 2xx + validation passed.
consumers: notifications::DispatchMarketingProviderTestSucceededNotification (opt-in to the tenant admin who ran the test)

events.name: MarketingProviderTestFailed
events.trigger: Test-mode dispatch returned 4xx/5xx OR validation failed. Signals a mis-configured credential / wrong pixel_id / invalid webhook_url.
consumers: notifications::DispatchMarketingProviderTestFailedNotification (cannot-opt-out — the tenant admin needs to fix the config before it goes live)
